amendments.body: `src/adapters/fs.rs:2957-2961` only auto-populates `plan_hash` when a brand-new start row is created; the completion path for an existing row still skips snapshot backfill entirely (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3231-3264`, and terminal replays use the same caller-only backfill helper at `src/adapters/fs.rs:2672-2692`). `record_bead_completion`/`update_task_run` also still forward `plan_hash` straight through from the caller (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:557-563`). The result is that any in-flight row that was created without `plan_hash` still finalizes with `plan_hash: None` even when the milestone snapshot already has the canonical hash. I reproduced this by pre-seeding a running `TaskRunEntry` with `plan_hash=None`, setting `snapshot.plan_hash="plan-v1"`, and then calling `record_bead_completion(..., plan_hash=None)`: the stored row remained `None`. That still loses lineage for legacy/incomplete starts, so issue 3 is only partially fixed.
amendments.rationale: The accepted fix was to auto-populate omitted `plan_hash` values from the milestone snapshot. Current code does that for new start rows, but not for completion-time writes, so previously-started attempts can still be permanently unlabeled.

payload.summary: Reviewed the milestone-lineage changes and ran targeted milestone-record tests around journal merging, runless-to-named completion, started_at guards, and plan-hash evolution; those passed. I found one remaining correctness gap: completion-time writes still do not backfill `plan_hash` from the milestone snapshot for pre-existing rows, so issue 3 is not fully closed.
